body:
This pull request extends support for running tests and deployments using both Elasticsearch and RDBMS as secondary storage backends for the Azure Kubernetes AKS single-region scenarios. It introduces a new RDBMS-based scenario, updates workflows and test logic to handle both storage types, and ensures that Elasticsearch and Optimize are only enabled and tested when relevant.

**Key changes:**

**Support for RDBMS as secondary storage:**
- Added a new scenario (`aks-single-region-rdbms`) to the test matrix and workflow triggers, enabling tests and deployments with RDBMS as the secondary storage backend. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-8864f7e7ef4065f55b829b48fa266eaad5c61396b612d729e262020b87406741R14-R18) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-e62872553a2f965a1db94fc5168c39a7753d0c017221f73ce4be2ac5b8996961R15) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-e62872553a2f965a1db94fc5168c39a7753d0c017221f73ce4be2ac5b8996961R48-R53) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-aad2c94c72af8bd257155018800a315e98bd7b821c9ca02e121f884c6a50c921R18-R22)
- Updated file triggers and workflow concurrency to include the new RDBMS scenario. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-e62872553a2f965a1db94fc5168c39a7753d0c017221f73ce4be2ac5b8996961L83-R90) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-aad2c94c72af8bd257155018800a315e98bd7b821c9ca02e121f884c6a50c921R18-R22)

**Conditional configuration for Elasticsearch and Optimize:**
- Modified workflow steps to only deploy and clean up Elasticsearch via ECK Operator when the scenario uses Elasticsearch as secondary storage. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-aad2c94c72af8bd257155018800a315e98bd7b821c9ca02e121f884c6a50c921L511-R508) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-aad2c94c72af8bd257155018800a315e98bd7b821c9ca02e121f884c6a50c921R624) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-aad2c94c72af8bd257155018800a315e98bd7b821c9ca02e121f884c6a50c921L759-R758) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-aad2c94c72af8bd257155018800a315e98bd7b821c9ca02e121f884c6a50c921L771-R770)
- Ensured that Optimize and its permissions are only enabled and tested when relevant, and are removed for RDBMS scenarios. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b4874455f39d0ddf18e2cf4e4bdfbc32ce598cf2bed402a67e4ff2573cf04142R220) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b4874455f39d0ddf18e2cf4e4bdfbc32ce598cf2bed402a67e4ff2573cf04142R351-R357) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-aad2c94c72af8bd257155018800a315e98bd7b821c9ca02e121f884c6a50c921R578-R583) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-aad2c94c72af8bd257155018800a315e98bd7b821c9ca02e121f884c6a50c921L732-R731) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-aad2c94c72af8bd257155018800a315e98bd7b821c9ca02e121f884c6a50c921L741-R740)

**Test and environment script improvements:**
- Centralized environment variable export logic by switching to a shared script (`gha-functions.sh`) for setting DB-related environment variables.
- Improved naming and reporting for integration test jobs to include scenario and declination details.

These changes ensure that the CI/CD workflows are flexible and robust for both Elasticsearch and RDBMS storage backends, and that resources and permissions are only provisioned as needed for each scenario.


Related to https://github.com/camunda/team-infrastructure-experience/issues/1064

commits.messageBody:
Add aks-single-region-rdbms reference architecture using PostgreSQL as
secondary storage for Operate and Tasklist instead of Elasticsearch.

- New directory azure/kubernetes/aks-single-region-rdbms/ with symlinks
  to shared files from aks-single-region and variant-specific files
  (db.tf, outputs.tf, helm values, manifests, procedures)
- Custom db.tf adds orchestration database for RDBMS secondary storage
- Helm values disable Elasticsearch/Optimize, enable RDBMS exporter
- CI test matrix updated with aks-single-region-rdbms scenario
- CI workflow conditionally skips ES/ECK deployment for RDBMS variant
- Golden workflow path triggers updated

Ref: team-infrastructure-experience#1064
commits.messageHeadline: feat(azure): add AKS RDBMS secondary storage variant

commits.messageBody:
- Update default postgres_version from 15 to 17 in aks-single-region
  variables.tf and postgres-db module
- Add renovate annotations (datasource=endoflife-date depName=postgresql)
  for automated version tracking
- Update READMEs to reflect new default
commits.messageHeadline: chore(azure): bump PostgreSQL default to 17 and add renovate annotations

commits.messageBody:
… golden test

- Convert test fixture symlinks to real files in aks-single-region-rdbms
  to fix 'Failed to read file' errors when CI copies fixtures with cp -r
  (GNU cp preserves symlinks, causing broken relative paths at destination)
- Add aks-single-region-rdbms to golden workflow matrix for plan comparison
commits.messageHeadline: fix(azure): convert test fixture symlinks to real files and add RDBMS…

commits.messageBody:
Identity CrashLoopBackOff in RDBMS variant because venom client
had optimize-api permissions but Optimize is disabled. The optimize-api
resource server doesn't exist in Keycloak, causing a NotFoundException.

Add identity-rdbms.yml without optimize-api and conditionally apply it
in the CI workflow when secondary_storage is rdbms.
commits.messageHeadline: fix(azure): use RDBMS-specific identity test values without optimize-api

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview Adds an AKS single-region **RDBMS variant** (PostgreSQL as secondary storage instead of Elasticsearch) and updates CI/golden workflows to test and generate plans for both variants. **Changes:** - Introduce `azure/kubernetes/aks-single-region-rdbms/` variant with RDBMS-specific Terraform/Helm values and DB setup procedures. - Bump PostgreSQL default version to `17` across Azure Postgres module + AKS single-region variables (and update golden plans/docs). - Update Azure AKS CI workflow matrix/logic to handle `secondary_storage: rdbms` (skip ECK/Optimize where appropriate) and add golden-plan coverage for the new variant. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 53 out of 53 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.

Zeebe topology in CI checks. | | azure/modules/postgres-db/variables.tf | Default Postgres version bumped to 17 with renovate metadata. | | azure/modules/postgres-db/README.md | Update module docs to reflect Postgres 17 default. | | azure/kubernetes/aks-single-region/variables.tf | Update AKS single-region default Postgres version to 17. | | .github/actions/internal-camunda-chart-tests/action.yml | Allow disabling Optimize tests via `optimize-enabled` input/environment. |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **generic/kubernetes/single-region/procedure/check-zeebe-cluster-topology.sh:23** * The topology fetch doesn’t fail fast on non-2xx responses (curl without `--fail/--fail-with-body`) and the script doesn’t validate that the response is valid JSON before writing it and running `jq`. This can result in `zeebe-topology.json` containing an error page/empty body while the script still exits successfully. Consider using `curl --fail-with-body` (or checking HTTP status) and `jq -e` (with an explicit exit on failure) to ensure the script fails when topology cannot be retrieved/parsed.
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 62 out of 62 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **azure/kubernetes/aks-single-region-rdbms/providers.tf:10** * This scenario uses `random_password` resources (see `db.tf`), but `required_providers` only declares `azurerm`. Terraform will auto-install `hashicorp/random` without a version constraint, which hurts reproducibility and can break golden tests when the random provider changes. Add `random` to `required_providers` with an explicit version constraint (and ensure the same is done in the shared AKS variant if it also uses `random_password`).
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

title: feat: aks rdbms variant and pg17 update
